clinicalSignificance.comment: Heterozygous frameshift variant in POLE producing premature termination at residue 54 of 2,286 amino acids. Eliminates all major catalytic domains including exonuclease (268-471) and polymerase (580-1260). Patient presents with ultra-hypermutated tumor phenotype (TMB >100 mut/Mb), progressive adenomatous polyposis (~6 adenomas at age 19, accumulating to ~15+ by age 31), Stage I papillary thyroid microcarcinoma (encapsulated, non-invasive, 0.6 cm, cystic variant with extensive squamous metaplasia; treated with total thyroidectomy), Stage IV+ deep infiltrating endometriosis, and congenital duplicated inferior vena cava. 47-gene hereditary cancer panel negative for all other genes — POLE c.138del is the sole identified genetic driver. Novel variant class for PPAP: all previously reported pathogenic variants are exonuclease domain missense mutations. Mechanism under active investigation (six candidate models). ACMG classification: PVS1 + PM2 + PP4 = Pathogenic.

condition.name: Colorectal cancer, susceptibility to, 12 (Polymerase proofreading-associated polyposis)

attribute.value: Female patient with progressive adenomatous polyposis (onset age 19), Stage I papillary thyroid microcarcinoma (encapsulated, non-invasive, 0.6 cm, cystic variant with extensive squamous metaplasia; age ~28; s/p total thyroidectomy), ultra-hypermutated tumor (TMB >100 mut/Mb), Stage IV+ deep infiltrating endometriosis with trans-diaphragmatic extension and pelvic free fluid, diffuse adenomyosis requiring ovarian-sparing total hysterectomy, bilateral proliferative breast changes with IHC-confirmed PASH (CD34+, CK AE1/AE3-), liver hypervascular lesion (hemangioma vs FNH, segment VII, 16x14 mm), congenital duplicated inferior vena cava, and ANA 1:160 with AC-21 pattern. Family history: maternal grandmother with uterine cancer and ductal breast cancer. Comorbidities: hEDS, POTS, gastroparesis. Negative for FILS syndrome, intellectual disability, and neurodevelopmental conditions.

assertionCriteria.citation: Richards S, et al. Standards and guidelines for the interpretation of sequence variants: a joint consensus recommendation of the ACMG and AMP. Genetics in Medicine. 2015;17(5):405-424.

_comment_lovd3: This variant has been submitted to LOVD3 (https://databases.lovd.nl/shared/variants/POLE) as of 2026-04-15, pending curation.
